Evidence: “RefreshQueue uses a per-tenant key: same-tenant duplicate refreshes coalesce, while different tenants continue in parallel.”

Concise explanation: `src/auth/queue.ts` deduplicates refresh work per tenant so duplicate refreshes for the same tenant are coalesced, but unrelated tenants are not blocked and can refresh in parallel. The rejected alternative was a single global mutex; message `auth_fact_2` explicitly says it was rejected because unrelated tenants must not block each other.
